steps.title: Find the graphed intersection
steps.content: The two equations already shown on the graph intersect at $(0,-6)$. For the three-equation system to have a solution, the added line must pass through that same point.

steps.title: Test the added line
steps.content: Substitute $(0,-6)$ into $x-6y=-36$. $$0-6(-6)=36$$ Since $36\ne -36$, the added line does not pass through the existing intersection. The three equations have zero common solutions. The answer is A.
